svg.text: Ethereum Consensus Mechanism: From PoW to PoS

g.data-narration: Proof of Work was Ethereum's original consensus. Miners competed using computational power to solve cryptographic puzzles and earn rewards for securing the network.
g.text: Miners compete to solve hash puzzles

g.data-narration: Proof of Stake replaced mining. Validators lock 32 ETH as collateral. They are randomly selected to propose and attest to blocks based on their staked amount.
The Beacon Chain is Ethereum's PoS coordination layer. It randomly selects validators, organizes them into committees, and manages the consensus process across the entire network.
g.text: Beacon Chain: The PoS Coordination Layer
Random validator selection via VRF | Committee attestations | Epoch = 32 slots (~6.4 min) for finality

g.data-narration: Slashing is Ethereum's security mechanism. Validators who act maliciously by double voting or surround voting lose part of their staked ETH as punishment.
g.text: Double voting or surround voting = penalty
Validators lose portion of 32 ETH stake

g.data-narration: Casper FFG provides finality. After two epochs (about 13 minutes), blocks become final and cannot be reverted unless over one-third of staked ETH is burned.
The Merge happened on September 15, 2022. Ethereum switched from PoW to PoS, combining the execution layer with the Beacon Chain consensus layer in the biggest upgrade ever.
g.text: Ethereum completed its transition from PoW to PoS, becoming the world's largest proof-of-stake blockchain

g.data-narration: Validators earn rewards for honest behavior: proposing blocks, attesting to blocks, and participating in sync committees. The more ETH staked, the higher the potential reward.
g.text: Block proposal | Attestations | Sync committee participation | Rewards distributed from protocol

g.data-narration: Ethereum solved the trilemma by choosing PoS. It achieved high security with low energy, maintained decentralization through thousands of validators, and improved scalability.
g.text: Security + Decentralization + Scalability: PoS provides all three through economic stake rather than computational work
